name: Elements
description: Modal synthesis voice: three exciters (Bow/Blow/Strike) feed a tuned resonator modelling plates, strings, tubes and bowls. Onboard reverb and stereo outputs. Two audio inputs let external signals be processed by the resonator.

controls.name: CONTOUR
controls.description: Built-in envelope applied to the BOW and BLOW exciters, triggered by PLAY or GATE.
controls.detail: Interpolates preset shapes: short AD → longer AD → ADSR → faster AR

controls.name: BOW
controls.description: Amount of bowed (scratching friction) excitation. More than a gain — simulates bow pressure, getting brighter and noisier as it opens.
controls.detail: Mixes with BLOW and STRIKE into the resonator

controls.name: BLOW
controls.description: Amount of blown (granular breath-noise) excitation sent to the resonator.

controls.name: STRIKE
controls.description: Amount of percussive impulse excitation. Past ~2 o'clock the strike signal also bleeds onto the output for a bitier transient.
controls.detail: Triggered by GATE edges or PLAY

controls.name: FLOW
controls.description: Air FLOW of the BLOW generator — wavetable scan through different colours of noise.
controls.detail: Modulate with an LFO for turbulent air-flow textures

controls.name: MALLET
controls.description: Type of percussive noise for STRIKE — morphs through sampled sticks/brushes/hammers, damped mallets, plectrums, and bouncing-particle models.
controls.detail: Full CW reaches physical models of particles bouncing on a surface

controls.name: BOW TIMBRE
controls.description: Smoothness vs granularity of the bow material.

controls.name: BLOW TIMBRE
controls.description: Pitch / granulation rate of the BLOW noise generator.

controls.name: STRIKE TIMBRE
controls.description: Brightness / speed of the percussive STRIKE excitation.

controls.name: PLAY
controls.description: Push-button that triggers the exciter and CONTOUR envelope. Equivalent to sending a positive gate to the GATE input.
controls.detail: Hold 5s (with specific knob positions) to enter calibration or switch firmware models

controls.name: COARSE
controls.description: Coarse frequency of the resonator, stepped in semitones.

controls.name: FINE
controls.description: Fine frequency tuning of the resonator.

controls.name: FM Attenuverter
controls.description: Amount and polarity of the FM CV input on resonator pitch. Exponential scale — very progressive attenuation near noon.

controls.name: GEOMETRY
controls.description: Geometry and stiffness of the resonating structure: plates → strings → bars/tubes → bells/bowls.
controls.detail: One sweet spot produces a purely harmonic spectrum (virtual notch)

controls.name: BRIGHTNESS
controls.description: Damping of high-frequency modes — material character, from wood/nylon (low) to glass/steel (high).

controls.name: DAMPING
controls.description: How quickly energy dissipates through the material — the decay time of the resonator.
controls.detail: Full CW sustains indefinitely (resonator self-oscillates under bowing)

controls.name: POSITION
controls.description: Point on the string/surface where excitation is applied. Middle position cancels even harmonics (square-like, hollow).
controls.detail: Analogous to PWM or comb-filter phasing; modulate for PWM-like motion

controls.name: SPACE
controls.description: Stereo width + reverb amount. Minimum: L = exciter, R = resonator. Rising: two-microphone stereo pickup of the resonator. Past noon: algorithmic reverberation of increasing size and decay.
controls.detail: Very high CV at max position triggers a freeze (infinite reverb tail)

controls.name: CV attenuverters
controls.description: Small attenuverters beside every exciter and resonator CV jack (plus V/OCT and STRENGTH).
controls.detail: Unity CW, invert CCW, zero at noon

inputs.name: GATE
inputs.description: Trigger/gate input for the exciter. Rising edge fires STRIKE impulse; CONTOUR envelope runs while gate is high, modulating BOW and BLOW.

inputs.name: V/OCT
inputs.description: 1V/octave pitch control for the resonator's fundamental frequency.

inputs.name: FM
inputs.description: Exponential FM of resonator pitch. Scaled by the FM attenuverter.

inputs.name: STRENGTH
inputs.description: VCA-style CV over the exciter signal level. Positive boosts, negative attenuates, ~3 dB/V, ±15 dB max.

inputs.name: BOW CV
inputs.description: CV input for BOW amount, scaled by its attenuverter.

inputs.name: BLOW CV
inputs.description: CV input for BLOW amount, scaled by its attenuverter.

inputs.name: STRIKE CV
inputs.description: CV input for STRIKE amount, scaled by its attenuverter.

inputs.name: SPACE CV
inputs.description: Modulates stereo width / reverb amount. Very high CV at max SPACE triggers reverb freeze.

inputs.name: EXT IN 1
inputs.description: External audio input routed through the BLOW signal path: diffuser → CONTOUR envelope → STRENGTH VCA → resonator.

inputs.name: EXT IN 2
inputs.description: External audio input sent directly to the resonator, unprocessed and without envelope or VCA.

outputs.name: OUT L
outputs.description: Left audio output. At SPACE min: raw exciter signal. Above that: left channel of the stereo pickup / reverb.

outputs.name: OUT R
outputs.description: Right audio output. At SPACE min: raw resonator signal. Above that: right channel of the stereo pickup / reverb.

behaviors.name: Three-exciter mixer
behaviors.trigger: any combination of BOW/BLOW/STRIKE with GATE or PLAY
behaviors.description: BOW, BLOW and STRIKE sum into the resonator. CONTOUR shapes the BOW/BLOW envelope; STRIKE fires on gate edges. Blend for hybrid articulations such as blown-and-bowed strings or plucked tubes.

behaviors.name: External audio through resonator
behaviors.trigger: audio into EXT IN 1 or EXT IN 2
behaviors.description: EXT IN 1 runs through the envelope and STRENGTH VCA before the resonator — good for shaping external audio with Elements' envelope. EXT IN 2 hits the resonator raw. Drums, voices, or pads become pitched modal material.

behaviors.name: Self-oscillating resonator
behaviors.trigger: DAMPING at max with steady BOW
behaviors.description: At DAMPING's top the resonator sustains energy indefinitely; a light bow keeps it singing as a nearly pure pitched voice. GEOMETRY and BRIGHTNESS sculpt the self-oscillating tone.

behaviors.name: Reverb freeze
behaviors.trigger: SPACE knob at max + large positive CV on SPACE
behaviors.description: Pushes the algorithmic reverb past unity feedback so its tail never decays. Useful for overdubbing layers of notes into chords or drones.

behaviors.name: Stereo exciter/resonator split
behaviors.description: At SPACE minimum, OUT L carries the raw exciter and OUT R the raw resonator — an insert point for external FX between the two. Raising SPACE merges them into a stereo pickup, then adds reverb past noon.

behaviors.name: POSITION pseudo-PWM
behaviors.description: POSITION at the midpoint cancels even harmonics (hollow, square-like tone). Modulating POSITION produces PWM-like comb-filter motion without amplitude artefacts.

behaviors.name: Firmware 1.1 alt resonator models
behaviors.trigger: all 8 resonator knobs + 5 resonator attenuverters fully CW, hold PLAY 5 s
behaviors.description: Cycles through three resonator models: 1 blink = original modal filter bank; 2 blinks = non-linear comb-filter string (GEOMETRY selects non-linearity type); 3 blinks = Chords, a bank of six comb filters tuned as a chord (GEOMETRY selects chord type).

behaviors.name: Ominous Voice easter egg
behaviors.trigger: all exciter attenuverters + all TIMBRE knobs MIN; all resonator attenuverters + DAMPING/POSITION/SPACE MAX; hold PLAY 5 s, then press PLAY
behaviors.description: Swaps the firmware for a 2×2-operator FM synth voice with stereo filter, autopan and reverb. Controls are repurposed (BOW = detuning, FLOW/MALLET = op ratios, TIMBRE knobs = feedback/FM amounts, GEOMETRY = filter response, BRIGHTNESS = cutoff, POSITION = rotation speed). Repeat procedure to return.

behaviors.name: Calibration mode
behaviors.trigger: disconnect CV; all attenuverters to minimum; hold PLAY 5 s; send 1 V then 3 V to V/OCT, pressing PLAY after each
behaviors.description: Two-point V/OCT calibration. Exciter LED blinks during step 1; resonator LED blinks during step 2. Move at least one attenuverter afterwards to avoid re-entering calibration by accident.

patchIdeas.name: Bowed string lead
patchIdeas.patch: Default resonator model · BOW ~2 o'clock · BLOW/STRIKE at 0 · GEOMETRY in the string zone · DAMPING ~2 o'clock · CONTOUR with some sustain · V/OCT + GATE from keyboard · take OUT L/R stereo
patchIdeas.diagram:
Elements:
* Model: Original (modal)

- Keyboard (Pitch) p> Elements (V/OCT)
- Keyboard (Gate) g> Elements (GATE)
- Elements (OUT L) -> Output (L)
- Elements (OUT R) -> Output (R)

patchIdeas.name: Drums through the resonator
patchIdeas.patch: Drum loop into EXT IN 2 (raw path) · all internal exciters at 0 · DAMPING medium · quantised pitch sequence on V/OCT · SPACE at 10 o'clock for a room · stereo out
patchIdeas.diagram:
Elements:
* BOW/BLOW/STRIKE: 0

- Drum Loop (Out) -> Elements (EXT IN 2)
- Sequencer (Pitch) p> Quantizer (In)
- Quantizer (Out) p> Elements (V/OCT)
- Elements (OUT L) -> Output (L)
- Elements (OUT R) -> Output (R)

patchIdeas.name: Breathy flute pad
patchIdeas.patch: BLOW ~3 o'clock · BOW/STRIKE at 0 · FLOW swept by slow LFO for turbulent air · GEOMETRY in the tube/bar zone · DAMPING CW · SPACE at 3 o'clock
patchIdeas.diagram:
Elements:
* GEOMETRY: tube zone

- Keyboard (Pitch) p> Elements (V/OCT)
- Keyboard (Gate) g> Elements (GATE)
- Slow LFO (Out) >> Elements (FLOW CV)
- Elements (OUT L) -> Output (L)
- Elements (OUT R) -> Output (R)

patchIdeas.name: Self-oscillating sine
patchIdeas.patch: DAMPING full CW · BOW at ~10 o'clock · BLOW/STRIKE at 0 · GATE held high (+5 V offset) so CONTOUR stays open · COARSE/FINE set the pitch like a VCO · GEOMETRY finds the harmonic notch for a clean tone
patchIdeas.diagram:
Elements:
* DAMPING: max

- Offset (+5V) g> Elements (GATE)
- Sequencer (Pitch) p> Elements (V/OCT)
- Elements (OUT L) -> Output (L)
- Elements (OUT R) -> Output (R)

patchIdeas.name: Hybrid percussion hit
patchIdeas.patch: STRIKE at 3 o'clock (bleeds onto output for bite) · MALLET ~noon · BOW ~9 o'clock for a scratched attack · short gates from clock · DAMPING ~11 o'clock · S&H into GEOMETRY CV for per-hit variation
patchIdeas.diagram:
Elements:

- Clock (Out) g> Elements (GATE)
- Sequencer (Pitch) p> Elements (V/OCT)
- S&H (Out) >> Elements (GEOMETRY CV)
- Elements (OUT L) -> Output (L)
- Elements (OUT R) -> Output (R)

patchIdeas.name: Pad through resonator (envelope path)
patchIdeas.patch: Pad synth into EXT IN 1 so it passes through CONTOUR + STRENGTH VCA · internal exciters at 0 · POSITION swept by slow LFO · SPACE at noon · V/OCT from keyboard · GATE from keyboard
patchIdeas.diagram:
Elements:
* BOW/BLOW/STRIKE: 0

- Pad Synth (Out) -> Elements (EXT IN 1)
- Keyboard (Pitch) p> Elements (V/OCT)
- Keyboard (Gate) g> Elements (GATE)
- Slow LFO (Out) >> Elements (POSITION CV)
- Elements (OUT L) -> Output (L)
- Elements (OUT R) -> Output (R)

patchIdeas.name: Reverb-freeze drone layers
patchIdeas.patch: Play a chord through Elements · SPACE at full CW · large positive CV pulse on SPACE to trigger freeze · overdub further notes into the frozen tail · BOW adds airy bed
patchIdeas.diagram:
Elements:
* SPACE: max

- Keyboard (Pitch) p> Elements (V/OCT)
- Keyboard (Gate) g> Elements (GATE)
- Envelope (Out) >> Elements (SPACE CV) // freeze pulse
- Elements (OUT L) -> Output (L)
- Elements (OUT R) -> Output (R)

patchIdeas.name: Chord-mode strums (firmware 1.1)
patchIdeas.patch: Switch firmware to model 3 (Chords — 3 blinks) · STRIKE short gates from a rhythmic clock · GEOMETRY CV from sequencer to step chord types · DAMPING medium · SPACE at 2 o'clock
patchIdeas.diagram:
Elements:
* Model: Chords (fw 1.1, 3 blinks)

- Clock (Out) g> Elements (GATE)
- Sequencer (Pitch) p> Elements (V/OCT)
- Sequencer (Aux) >> Elements (GEOMETRY CV) // chord select
- Elements (OUT L) -> Output (L)
- Elements (OUT R) -> Output (R)
